loggingOptions.description: The current settings of the IoT Analytics logging options.

roleArn.description: The ARN of the role that grants permission to IoT Analytics to perform logging.

level.description: The logging level. Currently, only ERROR is supported.

enabled.description: If true, logging is enabled for IoT Analytics.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

serviceManagedS3.description: Used to store channel data in an S3 bucket managed by IoT Analytics.

customerManagedS3.description: Used to store channel data in an S3 bucket that you manage.

bucket.description: The name of the S3 bucket in which channel data is stored.

keyPrefix.description: (Optional) The prefix used to create the keys of the channel data objects. Each object in an S3 bucket has a key that is its unique identifier within the bucket (each object in a bucket has exactly one key). The prefix must end with a forward slash (/).

roleArn.description: The ARN of the role that grants IoT Analytics permission to interact with your Amazon S3 resources.

lastUpdateTime.description: The last time the channel was updated.

lastMessageArrivalTime.description:
The last time when a new message arrived in the channel.

IoT Analytics updates this value at most once per minute for one channel. Hence, the lastMessageArrivalTime value is an approximation.

This feature only applies to messages that arrived in the data store after October 23, 2020.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

lastUpdateTime.description: The last time the dataset was updated.

triggers.description: A list of triggers. A trigger causes dataset content to be populated at a specified time interval or when another dataset is populated. The list of triggers can be empty or contain up to five DataSetTrigger objects

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

datastoreStorage.description: Where data in a data store is stored.

serviceManagedS3.description: Used to store data in an Amazon S3 bucket managed by IoT Analytics.

customerManagedS3.description: Used to store data in an Amazon S3 bucket managed by IoT Analytics.

bucket.description: The name of the Amazon S3 bucket where your data is stored.

keyPrefix.description: (Optional) The prefix used to create the keys of the data store data objects. Each object in an Amazon S3 bucket has a key that is its unique identifier in the bucket. Each object in a bucket has exactly one key. The prefix must end with a forward slash (/).

roleArn.description: The ARN of the role that grants IoT Analytics permission to interact with your Amazon S3 resources.

iotSiteWiseMultiLayerStorage.description: Used to store data used by IoT SiteWise in an Amazon S3 bucket that you manage.

customerManagedS3Storage.description: Used to store data used by IoT SiteWise in an Amazon S3 bucket that you manage.

bucket.description: The name of the Amazon S3 bucket where your data is stored.

keyPrefix.description: (Optional) The prefix used to create the keys of the data store data objects. Each object in an Amazon S3 bucket has a key that is its unique identifier in the bucket. Each object in a bucket has exactly one key. The prefix must end with a forward slash (/).

lastUpdateTime.description: The last time the data store was updated.

lastMessageArrivalTime.description:
The last time when a new message arrived in the data store.

IoT Analytics updates this value at most once per minute for Amazon Simple Storage Service one data store. Hence, the lastMessageArrivalTime value is an approximation.

This feature only applies to messages that arrived in the data store after October 23, 2020.

fileFormatType.description: The file format of the data in the data store.

datastorePartitions.description: Contains information about the partition dimensions in a data store.

partitions.description: A list of partition dimensions in a data store.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

reprocessingSummaries.description: A summary of information about the pipeline reprocessing.

batchPutMessageErrorEntries.description: A list of any errors encountered when sending the messages to the channel.

messageId.description: The ID of the message that caused the error. See the value corresponding to the messageId key in the message object.

retentionPeriod.description: How long, in days, message data is kept for the channel.

unlimited.description: If true, message data is kept indefinitely.

numberOfDays.description: The number of days that message data is kept. The unlimited parameter must be false.

retentionPeriod.description: How long, in days, dataset contents are kept for the dataset.

unlimited.description: If true, message data is kept indefinitely.

numberOfDays.description: The number of days that message data is kept. The unlimited parameter must be false.

versionId.description: The version ID of the dataset contents that are being created.

retentionPeriod.description: How long, in days, message data is kept for the data store.

unlimited.description: If true, message data is kept indefinitely.

numberOfDays.description: The number of days that message data is kept. The unlimited parameter must be false.

channel.description: An object that contains information about the channel.

statistics.description: Statistics about the channel. Included if the includeStatistics parameter is set to true in the request.

storage.description: Where channel data is stored. You can choose one of serviceManagedS3 or customerManagedS3 storage. If not specified, the default is serviceManagedS3. You can't change this storage option after the channel is created.

serviceManagedS3.description: Used to store channel data in an S3 bucket managed by IoT Analytics. You can't change the choice of S3 storage after the data store is created.

customerManagedS3.description: Used to store channel data in an S3 bucket that you manage. If customer managed storage is selected, the retentionPeriod parameter is ignored. You can't change the choice of S3 storage after the data store is created.

bucket.description: The name of the S3 bucket in which channel data is stored.

keyPrefix.description: (Optional) The prefix used to create the keys of the channel data objects. Each object in an S3 bucket has a key that is its unique identifier in the bucket. Each object in a bucket has exactly one key. The prefix must end with a forward slash (/).

roleArn.description: The ARN of the role that grants IoT Analytics permission to interact with your Amazon S3 resources.

retentionPeriod.description: How long, in days, message data is kept for the channel.

unlimited.description: If true, message data is kept indefinitely.

numberOfDays.description: The number of days that message data is kept. The unlimited parameter must be false.

lastMessageArrivalTime.description:
The last time when a new message arrived in the channel.

IoT Analytics updates this value at most once per minute for one channel. Hence, the lastMessageArrivalTime value is an approximation.

This feature only applies to messages that arrived in the data store after October 23, 2020.

dataset.description: An object that contains information about the dataset.

actions.description: The DatasetAction objects that automatically create the dataset contents.

triggers.description: The DatasetTrigger objects that specify when the dataset is automatically updated.

contentDeliveryRules.description: When dataset contents are created they are delivered to destinations specified here.

lastUpdateTime.description: The last time the dataset was updated.

retentionPeriod.description: Optional. How long, in days, message data is kept for the dataset.

unlimited.description: If true, message data is kept indefinitely.

numberOfDays.description: The number of days that message data is kept. The unlimited parameter must be false.

versioningConfiguration.description: Optional. How many versions of dataset contents are kept. If not specified or set to null, only the latest version plus the latest succeeded version (if they are different) are kept for the time period specified by the retentionPeriod parameter. For more information, see Keeping Multiple Versions of IoT Analytics datasets in the IoT Analytics User Guide.

unlimited.description: If true, unlimited versions of dataset contents are kept.

maxVersions.description: How many versions of dataset contents are kept. The unlimited parameter must be false.

lateDataRules.description: A list of data rules that send notifications to CloudWatch, when data arrives late. To specify lateDataRules, the dataset must use a DeltaTimer filter.

statistics.description: Additional statistical information about the data store. Included if the includeStatistics parameter is set to true in the request.

storage.description: Where data in a data store is stored.. You can choose serviceManagedS3 storage, customerManagedS3 storage, or iotSiteWiseMultiLayerStorage storage. The default is serviceManagedS3. You can't change the choice of Amazon S3 storage after your data store is created.

serviceManagedS3.description: Used to store data in an Amazon S3 bucket managed by IoT Analytics. You can't change the choice of Amazon S3 storage after your data store is created.

customerManagedS3.description: S3-customer-managed; When you choose customer-managed storage, the retentionPeriod parameter is ignored. You can't change the choice of Amazon S3 storage after your data store is created.

bucket.description: The name of the Amazon S3 bucket where your data is stored.

keyPrefix.description: (Optional) The prefix used to create the keys of the data store data objects. Each object in an Amazon S3 bucket has a key that is its unique identifier in the bucket. Each object in a bucket has exactly one key. The prefix must end with a forward slash (/).

roleArn.description: The ARN of the role that grants IoT Analytics permission to interact with your Amazon S3 resources.

iotSiteWiseMultiLayerStorage.description: Used to store data used by IoT SiteWise in an Amazon S3 bucket that you manage. You can't change the choice of Amazon S3 storage after your data store is created.

customerManagedS3Storage.description: Used to store data used by IoT SiteWise in an Amazon S3 bucket that you manage.

bucket.description: The name of the Amazon S3 bucket where your data is stored.

keyPrefix.description: (Optional) The prefix used to create the keys of the data store data objects. Each object in an Amazon S3 bucket has a key that is its unique identifier in the bucket. Each object in a bucket has exactly one key. The prefix must end with a forward slash (/).

status.description:
The status of a data store:

CREATING

The data store is being created.

ACTIVE

The data store has been created and can be used.

DELETING

The data store is being deleted.

retentionPeriod.description: How long, in days, message data is kept for the data store. When customerManagedS3 storage is selected, this parameter is ignored.

unlimited.description: If true, message data is kept indefinitely.

numberOfDays.description: The number of days that message data is kept. The unlimited parameter must be false.

lastUpdateTime.description: The last time the data store was updated.

lastMessageArrivalTime.description:
The last time when a new message arrived in the data store.

IoT Analytics updates this value at most once per minute for Amazon Simple Storage Service one data store. Hence, the lastMessageArrivalTime value is an approximation.

This feature only applies to messages that arrived in the data store after October 23, 2020.

fileFormatConfiguration.description:
Contains the configuration information of file formats. IoT Analytics data stores support JSON and Parquet.

The default file format is JSON. You can specify only one format.

You can't change the file format after you create the data store.

jsonConfiguration.description: Contains the configuration information of the JSON format.

parquetConfiguration.description: Contains the configuration information of the Parquet format.

columns.description:
Specifies one or more columns that store your data.

Each schema can have up to 100 columns. Each column can have up to 100 nested types.

datastorePartitions.description: Contains information about the partition dimensions in a data store.

partitions.description: A list of partition dimensions in a data store.

pipeline.description: A Pipeline object that contains information about the pipeline.

activities.description: The activities that perform transformations on the messages.

reprocessingSummaries.description: A summary of information about the pipeline reprocessing.

lastUpdateTime.description: The last time the pipeline was updated.

timestamp.description: The time when the request was made.

dataURI.description: The presigned URI of the dataset item.

datasetContentSummaries.description: Summary information about dataset contents that have been created.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

state.description: The state of the dataset contents. Can be one of READY, CREATING, SUCCEEDED, or FAILED.

reason.description: The reason the dataset contents are in this state.

creationTime.description: The actual time the creation of the dataset contents was started.

scheduleTime.description: The time the creation of the dataset contents was scheduled to start.

completionTime.description: The time the dataset content status was updated to SUCCEEDED or FAILED.

tags.description: The tags (metadata) that you have assigned to the resource.

payloads.description: The enriched or transformed sample message payloads as base64-encoded strings. (The results of running the pipeline activity on each input sample message payload, encoded in base64.)

logResult.description: In case the pipeline activity fails, the log message that is generated.

payloads.description: The list of message samples. Each sample message is returned as a base64-encoded string.

reprocessingId.description: The ID of the pipeline reprocessing activity that was started.
